initial_test_output:
=== TLS Chain Ordering Test ===
--- Building ---
--- Starting HTTPS server ---
Starting HTTPS server on :8443
--- Test 1: Lenient TLS client ---
OK: Lenient client connected successfully
--- Test 2: Chain order verification (strict RFC 5246) ---
FAIL: Chain order violation at position 0:
      cert[0].Issuer  = issuer=C=US, ST=Test, L=Test, O=TestIntermediateCA, CN=Test Intermediate CA
      cert[1].Subject = subject=C=US, ST=Test, L=Test, O=TestRootCA, CN=Test Root CA
      Expected these to match (cert[1] should be the issuer of cert[0])
FAIL: Chain order violation at position 1:
      cert[1].Issuer  = issuer=C=US, ST=Test, L=Test, O=TestRootCA, CN=Test Root CA
      cert[2].Subject = subject=C=US, ST=Test, L=Test, O=TestIntermediateCA, CN=Test Intermediate CA
      Expected these to match (cert[2] should be the issuer of cert[1])
--- Test 3: Leaf certificate identity ---
OK: First certificate is the leaf (CN=localhost)

FAIL: TLS chain ordering issues detected

cmd:
: "Reading README to understand the challenge"
cat README.md
output_preview:
# tls-chain-ordering-strict

## Project

A Go HTTPS server that loads a 3-level TLS certificate chain (Root CA -> Intermediate CA -> Leaf) from a PEM bundle and serves traffic on port 8443. The certificate chain is generated at Docker build time using OpenSSL with real RSA keys. The cert loader uses `tls.X509KeyPair` to match the private key to the leaf certificate.

## Symptoms

A lenient TLS client (curl with `--cacert`, Go's `net/http`, most browsers) connects to the server without errors. Th

output_preview:
FROM golang:1.22-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /build
COPY app/ .

RUN go build -o tlsserver .

FROM alpine:3.20

RUN apk add --no-cache bash coreutils git openssl curl && \
    git config --global user.email "bench@needle-bench.cc" && \
    git config --global user.name "needle-bench"

COPY --from=builder /build/tlsserver /usr/local/bin/tlsserver

# Install Go toolchain in final image so agent can rebuild
RUN apk add --no-cache go && \
    rm -rf /var/cache/apk/*

# Generate a 3-level certificate

 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("loading X509 key pair: %w", err)
 	}
 
+	// Sort the chain certificates (indices 1..n) so each cert[i] is

cmd:
: "The needle is likely the TLS certificate chain ordering bug. Let me verify by building and testing the current code"
docker build -t tls-test . 2>&1 | tail -20
output_preview: bash: line 2: docker: command not found

=== FIXED: TLS certificate chain ordering bug (the needle) ===
The bug was in app/tlssetup/loader.go - chain certificates were not sorted
Fix implemented: sortChainCerts() now properly orders certificates per RFC 5246

final_test_output:
=== TLS Chain Ordering Test ===
--- Building ---
--- Starting HTTPS server ---
Starting HTTPS server on :8443
--- Test 1: Lenient TLS client ---
OK: Lenient client connected successfully
--- Test 2: Chain order verification (strict RFC 5246) ---
OK: Chain is in correct RFC 5246 order
--- Test 3: Leaf certificate identity ---
OK: First certificate is the leaf (CN=localhost)

PASS: TLS chain is correctly ordered per RFC 5246
